title: asperula collina
intro: Asperula collina, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic to help with urinary problems. The leaves and flowers of Asperula collina have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to various health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. The herb can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies.
